excerpt: The three-month siege of Budapest engulfed 800,000 non-combatants.
transform_note: Denominator claim rather than a mortality count; schema placeholder uses unclassified_mortality_context.
confidence_note: Strong for headline trapped-population magnitude, though it is a rounded figure.

excerpt: In the city: 79,000 men (German and Hungarian defenders).
transform_note: Used as starting defender strength for denominator reconstruction.
confidence_note: Secondary summary; suitable as a starting-point denominator claim when used cautiously.

geographic_scope: Budapest city and immediate siege ring
excerpt: In the city: 177,000 men (Soviet and Romanian attackers).
transform_note: Used as starting attacker strength for denominator reconstruction.
confidence_note: Secondary summary; usable for bounding force exposure, not a detailed order-of-battle study.

excerpt: 38,000 died alongside a similar number of Hungarian and German military personnel.
transform_note: Used as the central estimate for Axis military deaths; low/high bounds widened later because 'similar number' implies approximation.
confidence_note: Strong single-source battle headline, but still rounded.

geographic_scope: Budapest city and immediate siege ring
excerpt: The casualties of the attacking Red Army, with some 80,000 dead...
transform_note: Used as central estimate; later low/high bounds widened because the phrase is explicitly approximate ('some 80,000').
confidence_note: Strong for headline scale, but rounded and not separated into Soviet versus Romanian components.

excerpt: 38,000 civilians died during the siege: about 13,000 from military action and 25,000 from starvation, disease and other causes.
transform_note: Context only; not used directly in core DR because the 25,000 category is mixed and includes indirect mortality.
confidence_note: Useful as a ceiling/context series, but not a clean direct-death numerator.

transform_note: Cleanest explicit direct-violent civilian subseries in the Budapest data.
confidence_note: Strongest clearly separable direct civilian-death component.

inflicting_side: Arrow Cross Party / Hungarian fascist militias
excerpt: 15,000 were killed in mass executions of Jews by the far-right Hungarian nationalist Arrow Cross Party.
transform_note: This indicates that a large share of Budapest civilian deaths were direct executions, but the same source also embeds executions inside a broader mixed 25,000 category; handled conservatively in the estimate stage.
confidence_note: Important direct-death component, but the decomposition around the broader 25,000 category remains methodologically awkward.

inflicting_side: Arrow Cross Party / Hungarian fascist militias
excerpt: Between November 1944 and February 1945, the Nyilas shot 10,000-15,000 Jews on the banks of the Danube.
transform_note: Broader than the strict siege window; used only to bound the Arrow Cross execution component conservatively.
confidence_note: Useful range source, but not perfectly episode-matched.
